title: South Korea
dek: Eight days based in Seoul — palaces, markets, gaming districts and street food — with a weekend in Daejeon for MSI 2026. Everything below is checked; tick items off as you go and your progress saves.
footer: Location photos via Wikimedia Commons — tap any credit for its source, author and license.
intro: Seoul base, with a two-night trip to Daejeon for the MSI 2026 weekend (Jul 11–12). Seoul in July is hot and humid with monsoon rain — pack light layers and an umbrella.
verified: Checked 28 Jun 2026 for the 8–15 Jul trip; base, transit, Air Premia baggage, shopping & send-off dinner re-verified 7 Jul 2026; Airbnb address (19 Jong-ro), booking total ($2,007.90), and A Flower Blossom on the Rice reservation confirmed 8 Jul 2026 · Tokyo side-trip section (third traveler, Jul 11–13) added & checked 10 Jul 2026 · Mon Busan restructured (KTX 10:44/18:00; east cluster — T1 Base Camp Haeundae + Bonjeon gukbap) + HYBE HQ into Tue shopping + Daejeon post-MSI beef (Yakiniku Doryong / Gobang) + Jongno reunion KBBQ, checked 12 Jul 2026 · Mon Busan re-plotted to subway (Line 1↔2 via Seomyeon, Haeundae Exit 5) with Gunam-ro lunch (Choryang Milmyeon / Obok dwaeji gukbap) and Seoul evening moved to Jongno-3-ga beef KBBQ (Ikseon-dong Ranch / Jongsam Yuk / Mido Galbi / Gwangju House) + pojangmacha at Exits 3–8, checked 13 Jul 2026 · ⚠ re-verify same-day before going: T1 Base Camp hours, Choryang Milmyeon / Obok hours, the Jongno-3-ga beef spots' late kitchens (several close ~22:30–23:00), Gobang/Yakiniku Doryong hours & Sunday opening, HYBE Weverse pop-up (may be none), KTX seats, Shibuya Sky/teamLab tickets & GO Fest roster · Citations recertified 17 Jul 2026 (post-trip): MangoPlate removed from the restaurant-apps section — the service shut down in 2020, so it was never live for this trip and should not have shipped; its write-up came from training data, not a check. Kakao T citation moved to Kakao’s official service page (t.kakao.com is gone). · Seoul orientation-map pins added 17 Jul 2026: 5 landmarks (Gyeongbokgung 경복궁, Gwangjang Market 광장시장, Myeongdong 명동, Dongdaemun Design Plaza 동대문디자인플라자, Hongdae/Hongik Univ. 홍익대학교) with coordinates, OSM place_ids, and native-script names all verified from OpenStreetMap Nominatim (name:ko round-trip-confirmed). Insadong omitted — native name did not verify cleanly, not guessed.

learnings.summary: The plan held where it was clustered and broke where it was exposed. Heat and rain — not fatigue — did the damage: both GO Fest park days were abandoned for remote raids from the hotel, and a downpour took out the Secret Garden and the Cheonggyecheon walk. Whenever a stop was merely far rather than blocked, proximity won every single time. 21 of 37 planned stops were hit; pace was the weakest part of the guide.
keyLearnings: A full-day outdoor commitment in July needs its indoor/remote fallback planned as a real branch, not a footnote — this group took the remote option on two consecutive days without hesitating once the heat hit.
Umbrellas were not enough — the group reported ending up soaked anyway, shoes included, day after day. A monsoon-season guide has to talk about footwear, not just rain cover.

Distance beat intent three separate times — Apgujeong lost to Myeongdong, HYBE HQ was dropped, and the Daejeon celebration grill lost to a closer strip. If it is not near the day's anchor, assume it will not happen.
A cheap, direct taxi beats a clever transit route. The Busan subway plan was skipped for a taxi the group found cheap and direct enough to settle it on the spot.
Kakao T fights a foreign phone number — bring a Korean e-SIM. Uber was the fallback, and it was worse (registration, traffic, heat).
Premium KTX seats are cheap but sell out days ahead; standing-room tickets are uncomfortable and not guaranteed. Book the moment the times are fixed.
Naver and Kakao Maps underperformed Google Maps and word-of-mouth for navigation and translation, and CatchTable missed many real venues — do not lean on any one app to find or reach a place.
Summer accommodation without AC is a real problem, not a minor comfort trade — verify it explicitly before booking.
changed: Recorded what actually happened on every day below, so the plan and the reality sit side by side rather than the guide pretending it went to script.

Promoted the durable lessons (heat fallbacks, rain gear, clustering, cheap-taxi-beats-transit, e-SIM for Kakao T, AC as a hard filter) into the traveler-patterns file the guide author consults, so the next guide starts from them instead of relearning them.

days.actually: Landed and went straight in on the 6002 Limousine bus — by the group's account the simplest, fastest way in. An intense downpour reshaped the evening: the Jongno walk became shelter and chimaek at a Korean fried-chicken place, then SPAREX earlier than planned.

skipped.reason: Skipped the shower; took the 6002 Limousine bus straight in as the simplest, fastest direct route.
Intense downpour and no rain-ready clothing or shoes — sheltered for chimaek instead, then went to SPAREX early.

skipped.stop: Dinner near the base — Jongno BBQ
skipped.reason: Walked the Jonggak restaurant list: many touristy, many closed by 10. Ended up at a slightly overpriced izakaya — but it was good.

days.actually: The palace day lost to weather and heat. Hongdae still happened, but for T1 Base Camp and KBBQ rather than raid hours.

skipped.reason: Too hot, too historical, and no time.
Went to Hongdae anyway — for T1 Base Camp and KBBQ instead of raids.

days.actually: GO Fest Day 1 never happened outdoors. The heat made in-person raiding untenable, so it became remote raids and a direct run to DCC II for MSI at 3pm plus the pre-MSI events.

skipped.stop: Daejeon Station → Kakao T to I-Hotel
skipped.reason: Used Uber instead — Kakao T registration fights a foreign number, and traffic plus heat made it unbearable. Get a Korean e-SIM; little translation available.

skipped.stop: EXPO Science Park — GO Fest Day 1
skipped.reason: Went straight to DCC II for MSI at 3 and did remote raids instead — too hot, few raids in person, and the pre-MSI events were on.

days.actually: The same call as Saturday, made again: stayed in the hotel for remote raids, ate at a burger chain before MSI. The celebration grill lost to distance and to how early Daejeon closes.

skipped.stop: EXPO Science Park — GO Fest Day 2
skipped.reason: Stayed in the hotel for remote raids — unbearable heat. Burger chain before MSI.

skipped.stop: Gobang Sikdang Expo Branch — celebration grill
skipped.reason: Too far. Went to a KBBQ strip near I-Hotel that barely had room, since most places close by 11.

days.actually: Busan worked. The taxi, not the subway, is what made it work — cheaper and more direct than the clever transit route. Lunch got skipped for time rather than because the plan was wrong.

skipped.reason: Took a taxi — very cheap and direct.
Not hungry, and little time to sit down.

days.actually: The big shopping day collapsed toward whatever was closest. Rain plus a group vote sent the morning to shops, and Myeongdong absorbed the day on proximity alone.

skipped.reason: Group vote went to shopping instead — rain as well.
Too far — did more shopping in Myeongdong for proximity and convenience, plus lunch.
